notes: Program list intentionally lightweight for MVP; migrate to broader EBKGC ingestion later.

programs.name: Carnegie Mellon University (METALS, OLI, OpenSimon)
programs.summary: Program and ecosystem focused on learning sciences, analytics, and learning engineering practice through METALS, OLI, and OpenSimon tools.

programs.name: Arizona State University (Learning Engineering Institute + Grad Pathways)
programs.summary: ASU’s Learning Engineering Institute and associated graduate pathways support research-to-practice learning system design at scale.

programs.name: Johns Hopkins University (LENS: Learning Engineering for Next-Generation Systems)
programs.summary: LENS is positioned as a systems-framing, decision-grade learning engineering pathway for high-consequence domains (defense, healthcare, and large-scale education).
programs.evidence_note: Program description aligned to user-provided draft: /Users/wgray13/Downloads/LENS_Prospective_Students_040726_draft.docx

programs.name: Boston College (Learning Design and Technology)
programs.summary: Graduate preparation path with strong ties to learning engineering methods and practice-oriented design workflows.

programs.name: ICICLE (IEEE SA Industry Consortium)
programs.summary: Consortium hub for shared resources, SIG/MIG practice communities, webinars, and conference-based field coordination.

programs.name: The Learning Agency Community
programs.summary: Community programming, resources, and networking support for practitioners and researchers applying learning engineering.

programs.name: Yet Analytics
programs.summary: Commercial tooling and services for data instrumentation and analysis workflows used in learning engineering settings.
